section_name: Passenger Seating Requirements for Certain Trips
chapter_title: Motor Transp. of Migrant Agricultural Workers

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A motor vehicle transporting migrant agricultural workers for a total distance of 100 miles or more must have a passenger compartment in accordance with this section.
be securely attached to the vehicle during use;
be not less than 16 or more than 19 inches above the floor;
be equipped with backrests that extend at least 36 inches above the floor;
have at least 24 inches of space between the backrests or the edges of the opposite seats when positioned face to face;
provide at least 18 inches of seat area for each passenger;
not have any cracks that are more than one-fourth inch wide;
not have any cracks in the backrests, if slatted, that are more than two inches wide; and
have any exposed wood surfaces planed or sanded smooth and free of splinters.

history.raw: Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 62, Sec. 17.34(a), eff. Sept. 1, 1999.
